description: Adversarial-survival prediction — would the claims hold up against §103 obviousness, §102 anticipation, IPR / EPO opposition / district court validity challenge? Inherently a Track A signal: requires comparison to ground-truth historical patents that survived adversarial review.
sub_criteria: Predicted survival probability based on Track A ground-truth comparison
Juristat API — claim-level prosecution outcome prediction
Lex Machina — litigation outcome priors for analogous claims

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: Independent claims align with ground-truth survived claims for the same disclosure (intersection-over-union of limitations ≥ 0.85); predicted IPR survival probability > 80% per Juristat / Lex Machina priors on analogous claim language; dependent claims provide multiple defensible fallback positions covering distinct technical embodiments.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Strong alignment with ground-truth survived claims (IOU ≥ 0.65). Minor scope deltas (an extra non-essential limitation in the candidate, a missing dependent claim covering a corner-case embodiment) would not change the adversarial outcome. Litigation priors on analogous claims trend favorable.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Partial overlap with ground truth (IOU 0.40–0.65); predicted survival 50–80%; litigation outcome priors are mixed across analogous claim language. Claim set would survive one IPR challenge but is exposed to specific lines of attack a motivated petitioner could exploit.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: Material divergence from ground-truth survived claims (IOU < 0.40); predicted survival 20–50%; analogous claim language has been invalidated in published IPR or district court decisions more often than maintained. Claim scope is either too broad (anticipated / obvious) or improperly narrow (missing key supporting limitations the original drafter included for a reason).

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Claim scope is anticipated or rendered obvious by a single cited reference; predicted survival < 20%; analogous claim language has been invalidated in the overwhelming majority of post-grant proceedings on the books. No defensible position against a motivated adversary at the PTAB or in district court.

track_a_signals: Independent-claim scope overlap (intersection-over-union) with survived claims
Juristat survival prediction score for the candidate's independent claim
track_b_protocol: Track B contributes minimally to Dim 3 — reviewers only flag claims that, in their professional judgment, would not survive an IPR they have personally seen. Default Track B score for Dim 3 is the median across reviewers; outliers (≥2-point deviation) trigger discussion before merge.
